frameworks.name: bistable perception
frameworks.one_line: Ambiguous images alternate between competing percepts with sudden switches

frameworks.name: perceptual memory trace
frameworks.one_line: Stabilizing trace bridging blank intervals during intermittent viewing

frameworks.name: neural oscillations (alpha/beta)
frameworks.one_line: Band-limited oscillatory power as a marker of perceptual state

frameworks.name: causal cortical stimulation (rIFG)
frameworks.one_line: Tests causal role of right inferior frontal gyrus in switches

core_question: Does rIFG causally drive bistable perception and how do oscillations track perceptual stability and memory?
key_assumptions: Bistable perception indexes mechanisms of natural visual stability
rIFG is a candidate node for perceptual switching control
Oscillatory power in alpha and beta bands indexes perceptual state
Intermittent presentation isolates a perceptual memory trace
manipulations: tDCS over right inferior frontal gyrus (double-blind cross-over)
Continuous vs intermittent presentation of ambiguous images
Tracking trial-by-trial fluctuations of oscillatory power
key_findings: No significant tDCS effect on perceptual behavior
Higher alpha and beta power predicted longer percept durations
Higher alpha and beta power predicted enhanced perceptual memory during intermittent viewing
interpretations: Alpha and beta oscillations track perceptual stability across continuous viewing
The same bands support a perceptual memory trace bridging blank intervals
limitations_noted: tDCS produced no detectable behavioral effect
